# Decision Log — [project name]

**Status: append-only.** New entries are added at the bottom, in
chronological order. Nothing above is ever edited or removed once
committed. A correction to a prior entry is itself a new, dated entry
that says what it corrects and why — it never edits the entry it
corrects, per `docs/RESEARCH_GOVERNANCE_STANDARD.md` Section 5.

This log is the single chronological record of every decision point
across this project's lifecycle: which candidate was ranked where and
why (Phase 2), which construction attempt was frozen and when (Phase
3–4), which freeze commit is in effect, which review occurred at which
level, and the final Decision (Phase 7).

---

## Entry template

Copy this block for every new entry. Every field is required; a field
with nothing to report still gets an explicit value (`None`,
`Not applicable`), never a silently omitted line.

```
### Entry [N] — [YYYY-MM-DD]

**Decision.** [What was decided, in one or two sentences.]

**Evidence references.** [File paths, commit hashes, or artifact names
this decision is based on.]

**Governance status.** [Which phase, gate, or control this entry
satisfies, per docs/RESEARCH_GOVERNANCE_STANDARD.md Section 2/8. If
this entry records a governance exception, state all three required
elements: documented reason, impact assessment, approval record.]

**Reviewer level.** [Level 1 / 2 / 3, per Standard Section 4. State
"Not applicable" only for entries that record no review, e.g. a
routine phase-transition log.]

**Known limitations.** [Anything a future reader should know about
this decision's own caveats or incompleteness. "None" is a valid,
explicit answer.]
```

---

### Entry 1 — [YYYY-MM-DD]

**Decision.** [Fill in.]

**Evidence references.** [Fill in.]

**Governance status.** [Fill in.]

**Reviewer level.** [Fill in.]

**Known limitations.** [Fill in.]
